verses.latin_vulgate: Homo natus de muliere, brevi vivens tempore,  repletur multis miseriis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭דָם יְל֣וּד אִשָּׁ֑ה קְצַ֥ר יָ֝מִ֗ים וּֽשְׂבַֽע־רֹֽגֶז׃
verses.douay_rheims: Man born of a woman, living for a short time, is filled with many miseries.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ihminen, vaimosta syntynyt, elää vähän aikaa, ja on täynnä levottomuutta,
verses.king_james: Man that is born of a woman is of few days, and full of trouble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui quasi flos egreditur et conteritur,  et fugit velut umbra, et numquam in eodem statu permanet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּצִ֣יץ יָ֭צָא וַיִּמָּ֑ל וַיִּבְרַ֥ח כַּ֝צֵּ֗ל וְלֹ֣א יַעֲמֽוֹד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who cometh forth like a flower, and is destroyed, and fleeth as a shadow, and never continueth in the same state.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kasvaa niinkuin kukkanen, ja kaatuu; pakenee niinkuin varjo, ja ei pysy.
verses.king_james: He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down: he fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dignum ducis super hujuscemodi aperire oculos tuos,  et adducere eum tecum in judicium ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַף־עַל־זֶ֭ה פָּקַ֣חְתָּ עֵינֶ֑ךָ וְאֹ֘תִ֤י תָבִ֖יא בְמִשְׁפָּ֣ט עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And dost thou think it meet to open thy eyes upon such an one, and to bring him into judgment with thee?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja senkaltaisen päälle sinä avaat silmäs, ja vedät minun kanssasi oikeuden eteen.
verses.king_james: And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis potest facere mundum de immundo conceptum semine ?  nonne tu qui solus es ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־יִתֵּ֣ן טָ֭הוֹר מִטָּמֵ֗א לֹ֣א אֶחָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who can make him clean that is conceived of unclean seed? is it not thou who only art?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka löytää puhtaan niiden seassa, kussa ei puhdasta ole?
verses.king_james: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.

verses.latin_vulgate: Breves dies hominis sunt :  numerus mensium ejus apud te est :  constituisti terminos ejus, qui præteriri non poterunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֥ם־חֲרוּצִ֨ים ׀ יָמָ֗יו מִֽסְפַּר־חֳדָשָׁ֥יו אִתָּ֑ךְ חֻקָּ֥ו עָ֝שִׂ֗יתָ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲבֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: The days of man are short, and the number of his months is with thee: thou hast appointed his bounds which cannot be passed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänellä on määrätty aika, hänen kuukauttensa luku on sinun tykönäs: sinä olet määrän asettanut hänen eteensä, jota ei hän taida käydä ylitse.
verses.king_james: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months are with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;

verses.latin_vulgate: Recede paululum ab eo, ut quiescat,  donec optata veniat, sicut mercenarii, dies ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁעֵ֣ה מֵעָלָ֣יו וְיֶחְדָּ֑ל עַד־יִ֝רְצֶ֗ה כְּשָׂכִ֥יר יוֹמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Depart a little from him, that he may rest until his wished for day come, as that of the hireling.
verses.finnish_biblia: Luovu hänestä, että hän sais levätä, niinkauvan kuin hänen aikansa tulee, jota hän odottaa niinkuin palkollinen.
verses.king_james: Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lignum habet spem :  si præcisum fuerit, rursum virescit,  et rami ejus pullulant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י יֵ֥שׁ לָעֵ֗ץ תִּ֫קְוָ֥ה אִֽם־יִ֭כָּרֵת וְע֣וֹד יַחֲלִ֑יף וְ֝יֹנַקְתּ֗וֹ לֹ֣א תֶחְדָּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: A tree hath hope: if it be cut, it growth green again, and the boughs thereof sprout.
verses.finnish_biblia: Puulla on toivo, ehkä se hakattaisiin, että se uudistetaan, ja sen vesat kasvavat jälleen:
verses.king_james: For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si senuerit in terra radix ejus,  et in pulvere emortuus fuerit truncus illius,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יַזְקִ֣ין בָּאָ֣רֶץ שׇׁרְשׁ֑וֹ וּ֝בֶעָפָ֗ר יָמ֥וּת גִּזְעֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: If its roots be old in the earth, and its stock be dead in the dust:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ehkä sen juuret vanhenevat maassa, ja kanto mätänee mullassa,
verses.king_james: Though the root thereof wax old in the earth, and the stock thereof die in the ground;

verses.latin_vulgate: ad odorem aquæ germinabit,  et faciet comam, quasi cum primum plantatum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵרֵ֣יחַ מַ֣יִם יַפְרִ֑חַ וְעָשָׂ֖ה קָצִ֣יר כְּמוֹ־נָֽטַע׃
verses.douay_rheims: At the scent of water, it shall spring, and bring forth leaves, as when it was first planted.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuitenkin se versoo jälleen veden märkyydestä, ja kasvaa niinkuin se istutettu olis.
verses.king_james: Yet through the scent of water it will bud, and bring forth boughs like a plant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo vero cum mortuus fuerit, et nudatus,  atque consumptus, ubi, quæso, est ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגֶ֣בֶר יָ֭מוּת וַֽיֶּחֱלָ֑שׁ וַיִּגְוַ֖ע אָדָ֣ם וְאַיּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But man when he shall be dead, and stripped and consumed, I pray you where is he?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kussa ihminen on, koska hän kuollut, hukkunut ja pois on?
verses.king_james: But man dieth, and wasteth away: yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where is he?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo si recedant aquæ de mari,  et fluvius vacuefactus arescat :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָֽזְלוּ־מַ֭יִם מִנִּי־יָ֑ם וְ֝נָהָ֗ר יֶחֱרַ֥ב וְיָבֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: As if the waters should depart out of the sea, and an emptied river should be dried up;
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin vesi juoksee ulos merestä, ja oja kureentuu ja kuivettuu,
verses.king_james: As the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up:

verses.latin_vulgate: sic homo, cum dormierit, non resurget :  donec atteratur cælum, non evigilabit,  nec consurget de somno suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֥ישׁ שָׁכַ֗ב וְֽלֹא־יָ֫ק֥וּם עַד־בִּלְתִּ֣י שָׁ֭מַיִם לֹ֣א יָקִ֑יצוּ וְלֹֽא־יֵ֝עֹ֗רוּ מִשְּׁנָתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: So man when he is fallen asleep shall not rise again; till the heavens be broken, he shall not awake, nor rise up out of his sleep.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin on ihminen, kuin hän kuollut on, ei hän nouse: niinkauvan kuin taivas pysyy, ei he virkoo eikä herää unestansa.
verses.king_james: So man lieth down, and riseth not: till the heavens be no more, they shall not awake, nor be raised out of their sleep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mihi hoc tribuat, ut in inferno protegas me,  et abscondas me donec pertranseat furor tuus,  et constituas mihi tempus in quo recorderis mei ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֤י יִתֵּ֨ן ׀ בִּשְׁא֬וֹל תַּצְפִּנֵ֗נִי תַּ֭סְתִּירֵנִי עַד־שׁ֣וּב אַפֶּ֑ךָ תָּ֤שִֽׁית־לִ֖י חֹ֣ק וְתִזְכְּרֵֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who will grant me this, that thou mayst protect me in hell, and hide me till thy wrath pass, and appoint me a time when thou wilt remember me?
verses.finnish_biblia: O jospa sinä minun hautaan kätkisit ja peittäisit minun, niinkauvan kuin vihas menis pois, ja asettaisit minulle määrän muistaakses minua.
verses.king_james: O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave, that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!

verses.latin_vulgate: Putasne mortuus homo rursum vivat ?  cunctis diebus quibus nunc milito, expecto  donec veniat immutatio mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יָמ֥וּת גֶּ֗בֶר הֲיִֽ֫חְיֶ֥ה כׇּל־יְמֵ֣י צְבָאִ֣י אֲיַחֵ֑ל עַד־בּ֝֗וֹא חֲלִיפָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Shall man that is dead, thinkest thou, live again? all the days in which I am now in warfare, I expect until my change come.
verses.finnish_biblia: Luuletkos kuolleen ihmisen tulevan eläväksi jälleen? Minä odotan joka päivä niinkauvan kuin minä sodin, siihenasti että minun muutteeni tulee,
verses.king_james: If a man die, shall he live again? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocabis me, et ego respondebo tibi :  operi manuum tuarum porriges dexteram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּ֭קְרָא וְאָנֹכִ֣י אֶעֱנֶ֑ךָּ לְֽמַעֲשֵׂ֖ה יָדֶ֣יךָ תִכְסֹֽף׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou shalt call me, and I will answer thee: to the work of thy hands thou shalt reach out thy right hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että sinä kutsuisit minua, ja minä vastaisin sinua, ja ettes hylkäisi käsialaas;
verses.king_james: Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu quidem gressus meos dinumerasti :  sed parce peccatis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה צְעָדַ֣י תִּסְפּ֑וֹר לֹֽא־תִ֝שְׁמֹ֗ר עַל־חַטָּאתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou indeed hast numbered my steps, but spare my sins.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä sinä olet jo lukenut kaikki minun askeleeni: etkös ota vaaria minun synneistäni?
verses.king_james: For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?

verses.latin_vulgate: Signasti quasi in sacculo delicta mea,  sed curasti iniquitatem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָתֻ֣ם בִּצְר֣וֹר פִּשְׁעִ֑י וַ֝תִּטְפֹּ֗ל עַל־עֲוֺנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast sealed up my offences as it were in a bag, but hast cured my iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun rikokseni olet sinä lukinnut lyhteesen, ja pannut kokoon minun vääryyteni.
verses.king_james: My transgression is sealed up in a bag, and thou sewest up mine iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mons cadens defluit,  et saxum transfertur de loco suo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭אוּלָם הַר־נוֹפֵ֣ל יִבּ֑וֹל וְ֝צ֗וּר יֶעְתַּ֥ק מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A mountain falling cometh to nought, and a rock is removed out of its place.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos tosin vuori kaatuu ja katoo, ja vaha siirtyy sialtansa,
verses.king_james: And surely the mountain falling cometh to nought, and the rock is removed out of his place.

verses.latin_vulgate: lapides excavant aquæ,  et alluvione paulatim terra consumitur :  et hominem ergo similiter perdes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲבָנִ֤ים ׀ שָׁ֥חֲקוּ מַ֗יִם תִּשְׁטֹֽף־סְפִיחֶ֥יהָ עֲפַר־אָ֑רֶץ וְתִקְוַ֖ת אֱנ֣וֹשׁ הֶאֱבַֽדְתָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Waters wear away the stones, and with inundation the ground by little and little is washed away: so in like manner thou shalt destroy man.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vesi kuluttaa kivet, ja virta vie maan pois, ja mitä sen päällä itse kasvaa: niin sinä myös ihmisen toivon kadotat.
verses.king_james: The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Roborasti eum paululum, ut in perpetuum transiret :  immutabis faciem ejus, et emittes eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּתְקְפֵ֣הוּ לָ֭נֶצַח וַֽיַּהֲלֹ֑ךְ מְשַׁנֶּ֥ה פָ֝נָ֗יו וַֽתְּשַׁלְּחֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast strengthened him for a little while, that he may pass away for ever: thou shalt change his face, and shalt send him away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet häntä voimallisempi ijankaikkisesti, ja hänen täytyy mennä pois. Sinä muutat hänen kasvonsa, ja annat hänen mennä.
verses.king_james: Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sive nobiles fuerint filii ejus,  sive ignobiles, non intelliget.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִכְבְּד֣וּ בָ֭נָיו וְלֹ֣א יֵדָ֑ע וְ֝יִצְעֲר֗וּ וְֽלֹא־יָבִ֥ין לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whether his children come to honour or dishonour, he shall not understand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ovatko hänen lapsensa kunnniassa, sitä ei hän tiedä, taikka ovatko he ylönkatseessa, sitä ei hän ymmärrä.
verses.king_james: His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attamen caro ejus, dum vivet, dolebit,  et anima illius super semetipso lugebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַךְ־בְּ֭שָׂרוֹ עָלָ֣יו יִכְאָ֑ב וְ֝נַפְשׁ֗וֹ עָלָ֥יו תֶּאֱבָֽל׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: But yet his flesh, while he shall live, shall have pain, and his soul shall mourn over him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuitenkin niinkauvan kuin hän kantaa lihaa, täytyy hänen olla vaivassa, ja niinkauvan kuin hänen sielunsa on hänessä, täytyy hänen murhetta kärsiä.
verses.king_james: But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.
